tif

Interface TifMetadataProvider

  • All Superinterfaces:
    java.lang.Iterable<DirectoryIFD>, Metadata<DirectoryIFD>
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    TifMetadata

    public interface TifMetadataProvider
extends Metadata<DirectoryIFD>
    Provides an specialised interface for accessing and managing TIFF-based metadata structures.

    This interface extends the base Metadata contract to support the unique hierarchical nature of TIFF files, allowing for the retrieval of specific Image File Directories (IFDs) such as EXIF, GPS, or Interoperability sub-directories.

    Beyond standard IFD structures, this provider also acts as a container for XmpDirectory segments, which are often embedded within TIFF and JPG files as XML-based metadata packets.

    Version:
    1.1
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs
    See Also:
    DirectoryIFD, DirectoryIdentifier

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void addXmpDirectory(XmpDirectory dir)
      Adds an XMP metadata directory.
      DirectoryIFD getDirectory(DirectoryIdentifier dirKey)
      Retrieves a specific Image File Directory (IFD) based on its identity.
      XmpDirectory getXmpDirectory()
      Returns the XMP metadata directory.

      • Methods inherited from interface common.Metadata

        addDirectory, extractDate, extractZonedDateTime, getByteOrder, hasExifData, hasMetadata, hasXmpData, isEmpty, removeDirectory

      • Methods inherited from interface java.lang.Iterable

        forEach, iterator, spliterator

    • Method Detail

      • getDirectory

        DirectoryIFD getDirectory(DirectoryIdentifier dirKey)
        Retrieves a specific Image File Directory (IFD) based on its identity.
        Parameters:
        dirKey - the DirectoryIdentifier representing the directory to find (i.e., IFD0, EXIF_SUBIFD, or GPS)
        Returns:
        the requested DirectoryIFD instance, or null if the directory does not exist in the current metadata set

      • addXmpDirectory

        void addXmpDirectory(XmpDirectory dir)
        Adds an XMP metadata directory.

        In TIFF-based formats, XMP is typically stored in a single global tag (ID 0x02BC). This method allows the parsed XMP object to be attached to the primary metadata container for consolidated access.

        Parameters:
        dir - the XmpDirectory containing parsed XML metadata

      • getXmpDirectory

        XmpDirectory getXmpDirectory()
        Returns the XMP metadata directory.
        Returns:
        the XmpDirectory instance, or null if no XMP data was found or added
